# Prerequisites {.unnumbered}

This part lists every account and piece of software the workshop needs, split by who needs it.

- @sec-learner-setup is the learner checklist. It is the pre-course work you hand to participants, and it is also the list every learner must complete before the day.

- @sec-teacher-setup is the teacher checklist. It is everything a learner needs, plus the extra accounts and setup that only the person running the course requires.

The two chapters overlap on purpose: a teacher needs the full learner setup too, because the best way to find a broken instruction is to follow it yourself as a learner (@sec-teacher-setup explains the test-learner account that makes this concrete).

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    subgraph L[Learner needs]
        GH[GitHub account]
        R[R and RStudio]
        G[Git]
        PAT[Personal access token]
        EL[Element / Matrix chat]
    end
    subgraph T[Teacher also needs]
        TL[A second 'test learner' GitHub account]
        OWN[Owner rights on the org]
        TWO[Two browsers or profiles]
    end
    L --> T
```

This handbook assumes a **local-first** setup: learners install R, RStudio, and Git on their own machines. A cloud option (Posit Cloud) is noted where relevant for groups that cannot install software, but the default path is local.

TODO: Why does the learner setup go first? I would put the teacher setup first, because the Chat channel, GitHub organization, survey forms, etc. need to be there before a learner profile could actually use them.
